rule: Before executing a route, cite the nearest method IDs, classify the work as replication/control, material modification, or genuinely different mechanism, and state the falsifiable delta. Replication is useful only as a control and cannot be promoted as novelty.

methods.mechanism: Delete three vertices from known order-42 graphs, extend the resulting order-39 graphs back to order 42, and iterate until no new class appears.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Recovered the same 656 known order-42 classes and reached closure under this process; none extends to order 43.
methods.material_delta_required: A sound extension decomposition covering inputs outside the known-class closure, or a certificate proving a materially broader exhaustive cover.

methods.mechanism: Start from a random order-30 Ramsey graph, append one vertex at a time, and repair with simulated annealing through order 42.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Generated 5,812 order-42 graphs; every result belonged to the same 656 classes.
methods.material_delta_required: A different information-generating mechanism with held-out matched-compute improvement, not another parameter tuning of annealing.

methods.mechanism: Use incremental vertex construction with tabu search instead of simulated annealing.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Constructed hundreds of order-42 graphs and found no new isomorphism class.
methods.material_delta_required: A new state representation or learned move block that wins on leakage-safe held-out classes against this baseline.

methods.mechanism: Bias stochastic search away from known order-42 graphs.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Reported attempts did not find a new class.
methods.material_delta_required: A canonical, measurable diversity objective with held-out evidence that it reaches basins absent from ordinary novelty bias.

methods.mechanism: Search neighborhoods defined by common edges or common subgraphs around known order-42 graphs.
methods.scope_and_outcome: More than one decade of CPU time found no new class.
methods.material_delta_required: A proof-carrying neighborhood cover or a nonlocal operator whose held-out behavior differs from common-edge/subgraph proximity.

methods.mechanism: Take 100 random order-36 subgraphs and extend them to order 42 in all possible ways.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Produced over 65 million order-42 graphs, all isomorphic to known classes.
methods.material_delta_required: A canonically distinct core family, a proved coverage expansion, or class-level blocking that avoids enumerating equivalent extensions.

methods.mechanism: Use circulant initialization and continuous-time MaxSAT dynamics.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Recovered order-42 solutions and found the published order-43 coloring with exactly two monochromatic K5s, but no zero-conflict K43 witness.
methods.material_delta_required: Beat burden two, prove a broader exact minimum, or demonstrate a held-out algorithmic improvement under matched compute.

methods.mechanism: Exploit cyclic edge-orbit structure and variations of Exoo-style lower-bound constructions.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Established the order-42 lower-bound construction lineage and later yielded low-conflict K43 variations; our certified one/two-orbit subfamilies remain at minimum burden two.
methods.material_delta_required: A structural theorem, a lower conflict count in a broader precise family, or a mechanism not equivalent to releasing more cyclic orbits.

methods.mechanism: Evolve Ramsey construction/search programs across random, algebraic, circulant, hybrid, fractal, and spectral initializations.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Improved several other Ramsey lower bounds and matched many known cells; it is a strong algorithmic baseline and method precedent, not an R(5,5) result.
methods.material_delta_required: Leakage-safe R55/order-42 held-out gains against the published algorithm families under matched evaluator calls and ablations.

methods.family: certificate-carrying SAT plus CAS and cube-and-conquer
methods.mechanism: Combine SAT with graph-symmetry CAS reasoning, parallel cubes, independently checkable leaf proofs, and exhaustive cover validation.
methods.scope_and_outcome: Verified R(3,8) and R(3,9) with major runtime gains over SAT-only; establishes the verification baseline for any R55 upper-bound reduction.
methods.material_delta_required: A deterministic R55 encoding/cover whose generator correspondence, symmetry coverage, and proof logs are independently checked.

methods.family: generalized SAT gluing with formal-verification intent
methods.mechanism: Decompose the R(5,5) upper-bound problem into generalized Ramsey subproblems, glue their SAT encodings, and pursue a proof-producing route suitable for formal checking.
methods.scope_and_outcome: This is active 2025 work aimed directly at R(5,5), not a completed new bound. It overlaps generalized block gluing, proof-core reuse, and certificate-oriented upper-bound plans in this campaign.
methods.material_delta_required: Before further scale-up, obtain author/artifact alignment and identify a decomposition, reusable lemma, proof object, or independent verification component not already being pursued in this program.

methods.family: structured strongly regular graph SAT exclusion
methods.mechanism: Attack the potential strongly regular graph srg(45,22,10,11), a structured R(5,5)-relevant case, using SAT and symmetry-aware computation.
methods.scope_and_outcome: The CMU/Mackey group reports an active structured SAT program around this order-45 case. It is a targeted subfamily, not by itself a complete R(5,5) upper-bound proof.
methods.material_delta_required: Coordinate on current status and artifacts, then contribute a separately checkable encoding, symmetry/coverage argument, certificate pipeline, or new structural reduction that the active effort values.

methods.family: proof-logged extension of the known order-42 corpus
methods.mechanism: Reproduce the barrier that the 656 known order-42 R(5,5) graph classes do not admit a one-vertex extension, retaining DRAT proof evidence and public experiment records.
methods.scope_and_outcome: BigCompute publicly reports a proof-logged reproduction of the known-class one-vertex-extension barrier and ongoing upper-bound exploration. This is independent verification of a known obstruction, not a new Ramsey bound.
methods.material_delta_required: Do not duplicate the same extension calculation except as a bounded control; require a broader exhaustive core family, a materially different verified decomposition, a new order-42 class, or an artifact/checker contribution coordinated with the project.

methods.mechanism: Integrate graph automorphism and canonicality reasoning into SAT search so symmetric candidates are rejected during solving rather than by labelled post-hoc blocking.
methods.scope_and_outcome: SAT Modulo Symmetries is an existing solver framework directly relevant to the campaign's canonical augmentation and repeated-isomorph bottleneck; using canonical blocking without comparing against it risks rediscovery of established machinery.
methods.material_delta_required: Benchmark against or build on SMS, state the exact soundness obligation for any custom canonical propagator, and show an R55-specific coverage, interoperability, or performance contribution beyond reimplementing generic symmetry rejection.

methods.family: independently checked SAT and formal proof certificates
methods.mechanism: Generate DRAT/LRAT or pseudo-Boolean proof objects, validate them with independently implemented checkers, and where practical replay the small trusted certificate layer in Lean, HOL4, or a verified CakeML checker.
methods.scope_and_outcome: DRAT-trim, LRAT, LRAT-Catcher, cake_lpr, HOL4 Ramsey developments, VeriPB, and the released SAT+CAS Ramsey artifacts establish a mature verification ecosystem. A locally replayed proof is strong evidence but is not novelty unless its mathematical scope or reusable verification contribution is new.
methods.material_delta_required: Bind every proof to exact generator/input hashes, independently check formula semantics and case coverage, disclose the trusted base, and demonstrate either a new mathematical exclusion or a verification artifact that an identified external effort will use.

methods.mechanism: Use neighborhood classifications and exact subgraph-counting identities to contradict hypothetical large Ramsey graphs.
methods.scope_and_outcome: This structural lineage underlies successive R(5,5) upper-bound improvements, including the current upper bound 46.
methods.material_delta_required: A new independently checked inequality/profile exclusion not subsumed by the published counting system.
